arguments.task: Open the WooCommerce REST API documentation and read the page content. Then separately open canonical official WooCommerce or WordPress developer/pricing/auth documentation pages needed to verify: what authentication methods are documented for WooCommerce REST API, whether the API is REST or GraphQL, breadth of the API surface, whether there is any official or clearly documented MCP server, and any official pricing or self-serve access information relevant to using the API. Return a concise structured summary with the exact page URLs actually visited and the key supporting statements from each page. If a page is JS-rendered, thin, inaccessible, or errors, note that explicitly and try another canonical first-party page.

input.thought: Use the browser tool to inspect official WooCommerce documentation and supporting first-party pages directly.

raw_result.description: Open-source ecommerce platform for WordPress with REST APIs for store, order, product, customer, and checkout data.
raw_result.auth_notes: WooCommerce REST API authentication docs describe OAuth 1.0a authorization for requests over HTTP and Basic Authentication over HTTPS using the consumer key as username and consumer secret as password. The docs also describe query-string authentication with consumer_key and consumer_secret, but that is mapped here under the documented Basic/OAuth patterns rather than as a separate schema value.
raw_result.gating_reason: WooCommerce pricing states the core platform is free and open source, and REST API keys are generated from a store's WooCommerce settings without partner or sales gating.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs expose multiple REST surfaces including the classic WooCommerce REST API (v1, v2, v3) and Store API endpoints covering products, orders, customers, coupons, reports, settings, shipping, taxes, webhooks, payment gateways, and cart/checkout flows.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official WooCommerce MCP server was verified from the gathered first-party pages; browser notes referenced an attempted GitHub docs path for MCP but no supporting official page content was captured, so this field is uncertain.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No first-party rate-limit policy was verified in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Docs are first-party and developer-oriented; WooCommerce has dedicated REST API and Store API documentation, API key generation in-admin, and free/open-source core distribution.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field api_surface.mcp_exists could not be supported from the captured first-party page content. No official MCP page was actually read to verify absence or presence, so this record should be reviewed for that field.

result.description: Open-source ecommerce platform for WordPress with REST APIs for store, order, product, customer, and checkout data.
